description: A GenServer runs slow work inside handle_call, so its serial loop backs up and callers time out under load.
body: Failure mode: a GenServer performs a slow external call, query, or CPU-heavy computation directly in handle_call/handle_cast, but the process handles messages one at a time, so the mailbox grows, every caller waits behind the slow one, and GenServer.call timeouts cascade across the system. Detection signals: Process.info shows a large message_queue_len; GenServer.call timeouts under load; latency scales with concurrency though the work is independent. Do not run slow work on the GenServer loop. Safer fallback: offload the work to a Task (Task.Supervisor) and reply asynchronously, use handle_continue for setup, or split the responsibility across processes/a pool, and load-test that the mailbox stays bounded.
